summary: twilio-sdk typosquats `twilio` and exfiltrates host + CI metadata on install
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate `twilio` SDK while shipping no functional code (`index.js` exports an empty object). On `npm install`, `postinstall.js` collects the installer's hostname, working-directory basename, Node version, configured npm registry, and CI repository identifiers from seven CI providers (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_PROJECT_USERNAME/REPONAME, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL) and transmits them as query parameters via cleartext HTTP GET to `http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping`. The destination is a bare IPv4 on a non-standard port with no TLS and no relationship to any legitimate Twilio infrastructure. Even without token values, the leaked data — private repo slugs, internal Jenkins/GitLab URLs, custom npm registry endpoints, host names — maps the installer's internal infrastructure and enables follow-up targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
